subject: IRDAI (Health Insurance) Regulations 2024
key_points: Insurers must settle or reject claims within 30 days of receiving all documents
Cashless authorization must be given within 60 minutes of request for planned hospitalization
Emergency cashless must be activated within 60 minutes
No sub-limit on room rent is allowed for policies issued after April 2024
All insurers must offer restoration of sum insured benefit
Pre-existing diseases must be covered after 36 months waiting period
AYUSH (Ayurveda, Yoga, Unani, Siddha, Homeopathy) treatment must be covered at government and NABH-accredited hospitals
Domiciliary hospitalization must be covered
Day care procedures (all IRDAI-listed procedures) must be covered
No policy can be denied at renewal solely due to claims history
Portability rights are guaranteed — new insurer must accept with accrued benefits
Mental illness treatment must be covered on par with physical illness
consumer_rights: Right to cashless treatment at network hospitals within prescribed timelines
Right to portability without losing accumulated No Claim Bonus and waiting period credits
Right to grievance redressal within 15 days (insurer's internal grievance cell)
Right to approach Insurance Ombudsman for disputes up to ₹30 lakh
Right to free look cancellation within 15 days (30 days for distance marketing)
Right to AYUSH treatment coverage
Right to no-room-rent-limit coverage in new policies
impact_summary: Most significant health insurance reform in India's regulatory history. Removes major customer pain points: room rent limits, delayed cashless, PED exclusions beyond 36 months.

subject: COVID-19 Coverage Mandates
key_points: All standard health insurance policies must cover COVID-19 hospitalization
Domiciliary (at-home) COVID treatment covered where hospitalization not possible
Specific COVID-19 Indemnity Policy (Corona Kavach) mandated for all health insurers
Corona Kavach: sum insured ₹50,000 to ₹5 lakh for COVID hospitalization
Corona Rakshak: lump sum benefit policy for COVID diagnosis
Waiting period of 15 days for both Corona products
Hospital daily cash benefit available for COVID hospitalization under health policies
No rejection of COVID claims citing 'epidemic/pandemic exclusion'
consumer_rights: Right to COVID hospitalization coverage under existing health policy
Right to domiciliary treatment coverage for COVID
Right to short-term specific COVID policy (Corona Kavach, Corona Rakshak)
impact_summary: Ensured no health insurer could deny COVID-19 claims using epidemic exclusion clauses. Created two specific affordable COVID products.

subject: Health Insurance Portability
key_points: Health insurance portability right guaranteed for all individual and family health policies
New insurer must give credit for waiting period already served with old insurer
No Claim Bonus accrued with old insurer must be honored
Pre-existing disease waiting period credit must be transferred
No fresh initial waiting period to be imposed by new insurer
Application for portability must be made 45 days before renewal
New insurer must inform of porting decision within 15 days of application
New insurer can reject porting but must give written reasons
Grievance available with IRDAI if porting rejected without valid reason
consumer_rights: Right to switch insurer without losing accumulated benefits
Right to carry forward waiting period credit
Right to carry forward No Claim Bonus
Right to written reason if porting rejected
impact_summary: Eliminated the 'insurance trap' where customers couldn't switch due to fear of losing waiting period credits. Increased competition among health insurers.

subject: Free Look Period — Mandatory 15 Days
key_points: All life and health insurance policies must offer minimum 15-day free look period
Policies sold through distance marketing (online, telephone): 30 days free look
Free look period starts from date of receipt of policy documents
During free look: policyholder can cancel for full refund minus proportionate risk premium
Proportionate deductions allowed: risk premium for days on cover, stamp duty
No other charges deductible during free look cancellation
Insurer must process free look refund within 15 days of receipt
For ULIPs: fund value at cancellation date refunded (not premium paid)
consumer_rights: Right to review policy document for 15-30 days after receipt
Right to cancel without penalty if policy doesn't match what was sold
Right to full refund minus proportionate risk premium
Right to protect against mis-selling by exercising free look right
impact_summary: Protects against mis-selling by giving customers time to review the actual policy document and cancel if misled. Critical consumer protection.

subject: IRDAI (Health Insurance) Regulations 2016
key_points: Maximum pre-existing disease waiting period: 48 months (later reduced to 36 by 2024 regulations)
Initial waiting period maximum: 30 days for accidental hospitalization (no waiting period)
Specific disease waiting period: 1-4 years for listed diseases
Day care procedure coverage mandated for all IRDAI-listed procedures
Portability must be offered by all health insurers
Annual renewal cannot be denied solely on claims history
Lifetime renewal guarantee mandated
Standard definition of pre-existing conditions must be followed
All exclusions must be prominently disclosed at time of sale
Premium loading for pre-existing conditions must be disclosed
consumer_rights: Right to lifetime renewal
Right to know all exclusions at time of purchase
Right to maximum 48-month PED waiting period
Right to day care procedure coverage
impact_summary: Foundation regulation for health insurance consumer protection. Standardized definitions and established key rights.

subject: ULIP Charge Caps — Reformed Framework
key_points: Premium Allocation Charge (PAC): maximum 3% in first year, 0% thereafter
Policy Administration Charge: maximum ₹500/month
Fund Management Charge: maximum 1.35% per annum of fund value
Mortality Charge: as per standard age-wise table
No surrender charge after 5 years
Minimum insurance cover: 10× annual premium for working age (46-55 years: 7×)
Net reduction in yield (total charges) must not exceed 4% for 10-year policies
Benefit Illustrations must show IRR after all charges
All charges must be disclosed upfront in policy document
consumer_rights: Right to capped charges on ULIP (FMC max 1.35%)
Right to benefit illustration showing net returns after all charges
Right to surrender after 5 years without exit charge
Right to know IRR at the time of purchase
impact_summary: Transformed ULIPs from high-charge products to reasonably-priced investment+insurance. Pre-2010 ULIPs had 60-80% first year charges; now capped at 3%.

subject: Standard Individual Term Life Insurance — Saral Jeevan Bima
key_points: All life insurers must offer Saral Jeevan Bima — standard term insurance product
Sum assured: ₹5 lakh to ₹25 lakh
Policy term: 5-40 years
Entry age: 18-65 years
Maturity age: maximum 70 years
Death benefit: sum assured in lump sum
Suicide exclusion only in first 12 months
No other exclusions allowed except suicide exclusion
No sub-limits, conditions, or loadings for pre-existing conditions beyond what's disclosed
consumer_rights: Right to a simple, no-frills term insurance without complicated conditions
Right to transparent product with no hidden exclusions
Right to affordable life cover from all authorized insurers
impact_summary: Democratized access to term insurance with a standardized, comparable product. Eliminated complicated exclusions that often led to claim rejection.

subject: Arogya Sanjeevani — Standard Individual Health Insurance
key_points: All health insurers must offer Arogya Sanjeevani — standard health insurance product
Sum insured: ₹1 lakh to ₹10 lakh
Co-payment: 5% of each claim
Room rent: 2% of sum insured per day; ICU 5% of sum insured per day
Coverage: hospitalization, day care, AYUSH, cataract, pre/post hospitalization
Waiting periods: 30-day initial, 2-4 years for PED and listed diseases
Standard exclusions list — no additional exclusions allowed
Policy available for individuals and family floater
Annual and multi-year (2-3 year) options available
No limit on maximum entry age
consumer_rights: Right to a standard, comparable health insurance product
Right to coverage from all authorized health insurers
Right to transparent terms with IRDAI-mandated standard features
impact_summary: Created a benchmark product enabling easy price comparison. Useful for underinsured segments and as a base comparison tool when evaluating comprehensive plans.

subject: Policyholder Protection — Surrender Value Rules
key_points: Guaranteed Surrender Value (GSV) mandatory after 2 years premium payment
GSV after 2 years: 30% of premiums paid excluding first-year premium
GSV after 3-7 years: minimum 50% of premiums paid
GSV after 7+ years: 50% or higher as per product design
Special Surrender Value (SSV) may be higher based on fund performance
Insurer must pay higher of GSV or SSV
Surrender value calculation must be transparent and disclosed
Paid-up policy option must be available after 3 years
consumer_rights: Right to minimum guaranteed surrender value after 2 years
Right to paid-up policy option after 3 years
Right to transparent surrender value calculation
impact_summary: Protected policyholders who need to exit long-term policies. Set minimum floor for surrender payments.

subject: Motor Insurance — Own Damage Claim Settlement
key_points: Survey for motor claims must be completed within 72 hours of intimation
Settlement must be made within 30 days of survey completion
For total loss or theft: settlement within 45 days after police NOC
Cashless motor repair available at empaneled garages
Insurer cannot demand repair at specific garage unless it's their empaneled garage
Pre-inspection of vehicle required only for annual policy issuance, not for claims
IDV cannot be unilaterally reduced by insurer — must be agreed at policy issuance
Depreciation rates standardized by IRDAI for claim settlement
Compulsory deductible: ₹1,000 for private cars, ₹2,000 for commercial vehicles
consumer_rights: Right to prompt survey within 72 hours
Right to settlement within 30 days
Right to cashless repair at network garages
Right to agreed IDV at policy issuance
impact_summary: Standardized motor claim timelines and prevented insurer abuse in IDV valuation at claim time.

subject: Mental Health Coverage Mandate
key_points: Following enactment of Mental Healthcare Act 2017, health insurers must cover mental illness
Mental illness hospitalization: covered on par with physical illness
No separate higher premium or exclusion allowed for mental illness
Coverage includes: severe depression requiring hospitalization, psychosis, severe anxiety disorders, addiction treatment
IRDAI issued guidance in 2019 mandating compliance
Discrimination against mental illness in insurance is illegal under Mental Healthcare Act 2017
consumer_rights: Right to mental illness coverage on par with physical illness
Right against discrimination in premium or exclusion on mental health grounds
impact_summary: Historic reform breaking the stigma in insurance for mental health. Mandatory coverage for serious mental illness in all health policies.

subject: Insurance Ombudsman Rules 2017
key_points: Insurance Ombudsman jurisdiction increased to claims up to ₹30 lakh
Ombudsman can investigate: claim rejection, partial settlement, delay, policy servicing issues
Process: policyholder must first exhaust insurer's internal grievance (wait 30 days), then approach Ombudsman
17 Ombudsman offices across India
Filing: online at ecoi.co.in or physical form
No fee for filing complaint
Award binding on insurer if up to ₹30 lakh
Ombudsman decision within 3 months
Policyholder can approach Consumer Forum for claims above ₹30 lakh
Ombudsman can also award interest and costs against insurer
consumer_rights: Free grievance redressal for disputes up to ₹30 lakh
Binding award against insurer
Right to interest on delayed settlement
impact_summary: Accessible, fast, and free dispute resolution for insurance policyholders. Reduced need for costly litigation.

subject: Senior Citizen Health Insurance — Restrictions on Refusal
key_points: Insurers cannot refuse health insurance solely on grounds of age
No upper age limit for insurance renewal for existing policyholders
New senior citizen policies must be offered without blanket age exclusions
Copay for senior citizens: maximum 30% per hospitalization
Pre-existing disease waiting period: maximum 48 months even for senior citizens
Annual health check required and covered for senior citizens on renewal
Premium increases for seniors must be reasonable and actuarially justified
Senior citizens have right to switch insurer (portability) even for first-time buyers above 65
consumer_rights: Right to health insurance regardless of age
Right to lifetime renewal
Right to portability as senior citizen
Right to annual health check coverage
impact_summary: Addressed India's senior citizen health insurance gap. Senior citizens (65+) can now access individual health insurance without blanket exclusions.

subject: Life Insurance Claim Settlement — 30-Day Rule
key_points: Life insurance death claims: insurer must investigate and settle within 30 days of receiving all documents
Claims pending investigation: maximum extension of 90 days with written communication to nominee
After 90 days, insurer must pay interest on delayed claim at 2% above bank rate
Early claim (death within 3 years of policy): insurer can investigate but must complete within 90 days
Non-early claim (death after 3 years): no investigation allowed unless fraud evidence — must settle within 30 days
Repudiation of claim must give written reason with specific policy clause cited
Nominee has right to written rejection with grounds
Grievance available at IRDAI, Ombudsman, and Consumer Courts
consumer_rights: Right to claim settlement within 30 days
Right to interest on delayed settlement (2% above bank rate)
Right to written rejection with specific grounds cited
Right to speedy settlement for non-early claims (post-3-years)
impact_summary: Eliminated indefinite claim delays. Mandatory timelines with interest penalties for insurers.

subject: Travel Insurance — COVID and Overseas Medical Coverage
key_points: Travel insurance must cover COVID-19 hospitalization during international travel
Medical evacuation/repatriation coverage mandated
Overseas emergency medical expenses: minimum coverage thresholds set
Trip cancellation due to medical emergency: covered
Passport loss: covered
Travel delay (8+ hours): daily cash benefit mandated
Pre-existing disease coverage during travel: available as add-on
Claims settlement for overseas claims: 30 days from document receipt
consumer_rights: Right to COVID coverage during overseas travel
Right to emergency medical evacuation coverage
Right to trip cancellation for medical emergencies
impact_summary: Standardized travel insurance during and post-COVID era. Ensures travelers have meaningful coverage.

subject: Cashless Claim Processing — Enhanced Timelines
key_points: Cashless pre-authorization for planned hospitalization: within 2 hours
Emergency cashless: within 60 minutes of request
Final authorization at discharge: within 3 hours
If authorization delayed beyond mandated time: insurer must allow reimbursement claim
Network hospital cannot refuse to process cashless due to insurer delay
In case of cashless denial: written reason with policy clause must be given
TPA performance standards monitored by IRDAI
Insurer/TPA helpline must be available 24×7
Digital pre-authorization forms accepted
consumer_rights: Right to cashless authorization within 2 hours (planned) or 60 minutes (emergency)
Right to reimbursement if cashless delayed due to insurer
Right to 24×7 helpline access
Right to written denial with specific grounds
impact_summary: Addressed the biggest cashless pain point: authorization delays causing patients to pay upfront at network hospitals.

subject: Prohibition of Life Insurance Mis-selling
key_points: Insurers and agents cannot use misleading illustrations to sell policies
Benefit Illustration at 4% and 8% scenario mandatory for traditional policies
IRR must be disclosed in benefit illustration
For ULIPs: return scenarios at 4%, 8% must be shown; actual returns not guaranteed
Agents cannot promise guaranteed returns on market-linked products (ULIPs)
Complaints about mis-selling: can approach IRDAI, Ombudsman
Agents/insurers found mis-selling face license cancellation and monetary penalty
Cooling-off period enhanced to strengthen free look rights
consumer_rights: Right to accurate benefit illustration with realistic return scenarios
Right to IRR disclosure
Right to file mis-selling complaint with IRDAI
Right to free look cancellation if policy misrepresented
impact_summary: Strengthened consumer protection against the rampant mis-selling of ULIPs and traditional policies in India.

subject: Multi-Year Motor Insurance — Third Party
key_points: Third party insurance for new private cars: mandatory 3-year policy from inception
Third party insurance for new two-wheelers: mandatory 5-year policy from inception
Own damage insurance: annual policy continues (separate from TP)
Bundled product: TP (3 or 5 years) + OD (1 year) sold together for new vehicles
Premium for multi-year TP cannot be revised during the term
On total loss/theft, refund of pro-rated TP premium
Separate renewal of OD and TP allowed after initial bundled period
consumer_rights: Right to locked-in third-party premium for 3-5 years
Right to separate own damage renewal
Right to pro-rated TP refund on total loss
impact_summary: Eliminated the problem of lapsed TP insurance on new vehicles. Reduced road accidents caused by uninsured vehicles.

subject: Bima Sugam — Insurance Digital Marketplace
key_points: Bima Sugam: IRDAI's digital marketplace for buying, servicing, and claiming insurance
Single platform for comparing and buying life, health, and general insurance
Electronic Insurance Account (e-IA) for all policyholders
All policies stored in digital repository (Insurance Repository)
Claims initiation and tracking on Bima Sugam
Integration with DigiLocker for KYC
IRDAI's vision: 'Insurance for All by 2047'
consumer_rights: Right to transparent product comparison on single platform
Right to digital policy repository
Right to digital claims processing
impact_summary: IRDAI's flagship initiative to digitize and democratize insurance access. Long-term transformation of how Indians buy and manage insurance.

subject: Health Insurance — Standardization of Common Definitions
key_points: Standard definition of 'Pre-existing Disease': any condition for which treatment/advice received in 48 months before policy start
Standard definition of 'Hospitalization': minimum 24 hours admission; exceptions for day care procedures
Standard definition of 'Day Care Treatment': IRDAI-listed 408+ procedures covered without 24-hour requirement
Standard definition of 'Reasonable and Customary Charges': expenses not exceeding standard charges for same service in same geography
Standard definition of 'Co-payment': fixed percentage of each claim borne by policyholder
Standard definition of 'Network Hospital': hospital with direct billing agreement with insurer/TPA
All health policies must use these standardized definitions — no deviations allowed
consumer_rights: Right to standardized policy terms
Right to clear definition of pre-existing disease
Right to day care procedure coverage per IRDAI list
impact_summary: Eliminated definitional ambiguity that insurers exploited to reject claims. Created level playing field for policyholders.
